claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in the 20-week extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in the 20-week extension period; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in the 20-week extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with a few basic questions:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for your child, I need to ask you a few questions.

**What is your child's name and how old are they?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child's name is Alex and they're 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 17 years old, their eligibility may depend on whether they're in education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, they actually left school recently, but they've just signed up with the careers service to figure out what they want to do next.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful information. Since Alex is 17, has left education, and has registered with a careers service, you may be able to continue receiving Child Benefit for a limited period.

Let me ask a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your relationship with Alex to determine responsibility:

**Do you live with Alex, or if not, do you contribute financially towards their upkeep (such as food, clothes, or pocket money)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for that information. Just one final question to complete my assessment:

**Has anyone else already claimed Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's the summary of your situation:

- Alex is 17 years old and has recently left education
- Because Alex has registered with the careers service, you can continue to receive Child Benefit for **up to 20 weeks**
- You meet all the other requirements: you live in the UK, Alex lives with you (which establishes you as responsible for them), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

**Important to note:** This eligibility is for a 20-week period while Alex is registered with the careers service. If Alex starts approved education or training during this time, your Child Benefit can continue. However, your Child Benefit will stop if Alex:
- Starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week (and is not in approved education or training)
- Starts an apprenticeship in England
- Starts getting certain benefits like Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit

If you haven't already claimed, you should apply for Child Benefit. If you're already receiving it, you should inform HMRC about Alex's current situation with the careers service to ensure your payments continue correctly.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and has recently left education or training. According to the rules, you can apply for Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training and registers with a government-sponsored careers service. Alex has registered with the careers service, which qualifies. The claimant lives in the UK, lives with Alex (establishing responsibility for the child), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex for up to 20 weeks following their registration with the careers service.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 17-year-old child Alex. Although Alex has left education, they have registered with a careers service, which allows Child Benefit to continue for up to 20 weeks. The claimant meets all other requirements: they live in the UK, live with and are responsible for Alex, and no one else is claiming for this child.
